channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BBC appoints former Google executive Matt Brittin as director general amid legal and funding challenges
item.description: The BBC has announced Matt Brittin, a former Google executive, as its new director general, taking over from Tim Davie, who resigned last year after controversy over an edited speech by former US Pres...

item.title: Gold Prices Drop Amid Middle East Conflict and Global Market Pressures
item.description: Despite gold’s reputation as a safe-haven asset, the price has seen a sharp decline this year following the outbreak of the Middle East war. Investors, facing heightened uncertainty and the need for c...

item.title: Iran Rejects US Peace Proposal, Lists 5 Conditions for Ending the War
item.description: Iran has firmly rejected a proposal from the United States aimed at ending the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran. According to a report from Iran's state-run Press TV, Tehran is determ...

item.title: South Korean prisoner serving life in the Philippines temporarily extradited over drug trafficking charges
item.description: South Korean authorities have taken into custody Park Wang-yeol, a 47-year-old man accused of leading a major drug trafficking operation from inside the New Bilibid Prison in Muntinlupa City, Philippi...

item.title: Knowledge Channel Partners with Cable Operators to Broaden DepEd TV Coverage Across the Philippines
item.description: In a significant move to improve the accessibility of educational content across the country, Knowledge Channel has partnered with major cable operators to expand the reach of DepEd TV. This collabora...

item.title: NDMA Issues Warning for Rain and Thunderstorms Across Pakistan in Next 24 Hours
item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued an advisory forecasting rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds across Pakistan in the next 24 hours. A western wind system from Balochistan ...

item.title: Sri Lanka Adopts Four-Day Workweek Due to Energy Crisis Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Sri Lanka has introduced a government-mandated four-day workweek to tackle an ongoing energy crisis that has worsened due to the war in the Middle East. The conflict, particularly the US-Israel bombin...

item.title: China Restricts Manus Cofounders Amid Investigation of Meta Acquisition
item.description: China has imposed travel restrictions on the cofounders of Manus, an AI startup acquired by Meta in late 2025 for an estimated $2 to $3 billion. Xiao Hong, Manus' CEO, and Ji Yichao, its chief scienti...

item.title: Usher Reflects on His Respect for Diddy Despite Controversies
item.description: In a recent interview with Forbes’ The Enterprise Zone, Usher Raymond, 47, spoke candidly about his ongoing respect for Sean 'Diddy' Combs, 56, despite the public controversies surrounding him. Usher ...

item.title: Gamondi sticks to AFCON squad, brings in fresh talent for future growth
item.description: Miguel Gamondi, the head coach of Tanzania's national football team, Taifa Stars, has confirmed his commitment to building a competitive future by mixing experience with young talent. As preparations ...

item.title: Study warns Pakistani cities face severe rise in heat-related deaths by 2050 due to lack of adaptation planning
item.description: A new global study by the Climate Impact Lab at the University of Chicago has projected a sharp increase in deaths linked to rising temperatures, with Pakistan among the hardest-hit countries. By 2050...

item.title: Govt finalises app-based fuel quota system for motorcycles, rickshaws to ease oil crisis impact
item.description: The Pakistani government has finalised a mobile app-based fuel quota system for motorcycles and rickshaws, with the possibility of extending it to small cars. This system aims to target subsidies to l...

item.title: Memories, Remedies and Local Anecdotes from Across Australia
item.description: This Column 8 article compiles quirky, humorous, and nostalgic stories from readers around Australia. Margaret Grove recalls a visit to a pub in Baradine, where the wine options were simply red or whi...

item.title: Liverpool searching for top winger to replace Mohamed Salah
item.description: Mohamed Salah has confirmed he will leave Liverpool at the end of the 2025/26 season, bringing an end to a glorious chapter in the club’s history. The 33-year-old, who joined Liverpool from AS Roma in...

item.title: North American Jewish communities increase security amid heightened global terror threats
item.description: Since the Hamas attack in Israel on October 7, 2023, Jewish communities in North America have been on heightened alert due to a notable surge in antisemitic incidents. Recent weeks have seen multiple ...

item.title: US Organization and Rungwe College Boost Tourism by Promoting Local Attractions to American Tourists
item.description: In a bid to increase tourism in Rungwe District, the Teaching Artist Institute (TAI), a US-based organization, has partnered with Rungwe International College of Business and Entrepreneurship Developm...

item.title: Riz Ahmed’s Bait: A Sharp, Humorous Take on Fame and Family
item.description: Bait is a clever six-episode series created by Riz Ahmed, blending semi-autobiographical storytelling with biting industry satire. Ahmed stars as Shah Latif, a rapper-turned-actor from a lively West L...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM criticises Centre for focusing on delimitation amid cooking gas crisis
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin has accused the BJP-led Centre of using the issue of delimitation to divert public attention away from the ongoing cooking gas shortage. Stalin stated that the sh...

item.title: Tanzania Signs Niobium Deal to Boost Mineral Production and Trade
item.description: Tanzania has signed a significant deal with Panda Hill Tanzania Limited, a US-owned company, to boost the production and processing of niobium, a strategic metal critical for steel strengthening and h...

item.title: Gatchalian pushes for coordinated national plan to manage rising oil prices
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian has urged the Philippine government to develop a unified national contingency plan in response to the ongoing oil crisis. Rising oil prices, influenced by global tensions in...
